            if k in ALLOWED:
                fields[k] = v.strip()
        if fields.get("name"):
            workflows.append(fields)
    return workflows


# в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ Ollama в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

def mcp_to_ollama_tools(mcp_tools) -> list:
    return [
        {
            "type": "function",
            "function": {
                "name":        t.name,
                "description": t.description or "",
                "parameters":  t.inputSchema or {"type": "object", "properties": {}},
            },
        }
        for t in mcp_tools
    ]


async def ollama_chat(messages: list, tools: list, client: httpx.AsyncClient) -> dict:
    payload = {
        "model":    OLLAMA_MODEL,
        "messages": messages,
        "stream":   False,
        "options":  {"num_gpu": 35, "num_thread": 8, "num_ctx": 2048},
    }
    if tools:
        payload["tools"] = tools
    r = await client.post(OLLAMA_URL, json=payload, timeout=300.0)
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.json()["message"]


# в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ Queue runner в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

async def run_queue(session: ClientSession, stop_after: str = "S4") -> dict:
    workflows = queue_load()
    if not workflows:
        print(f"{C.YELLOW}  Queue is empty.{C.RESET}\n")
        return {"status": "error", "error": "Queue is empty"}

    total = len(workflows)
    print(f"\n{C.PURPLE}  Running {total} workflows one by one (stop after {stop_after})...{C.RESET}\n")

    results = []
    for i, wf in enumerate(workflows):
        name = wf.get("name", f"Workflow {i+1}")
        print(f"{C.CYAN}  [{i+1}/{total}] Creating: {name}{C.RESET}")

        try:
            r = await session.call_tool("shani_create_workflow", wf)
            data = json.loads(r.content[0].text)
        except Exception as exc:
            print(f"{C.RED}    Create error: {exc}{C.RESET}")
            results.append({"name": name, "status": "error"})
            continue

        # Success if workflow_id present (SHANI returns "paused" not "success")
        if "workflow_id" not in data and data.get("status") == "error":
            print(f"{C.RED}    Create failed: {data.get('error')}{C.RESET}")
            results.append({"name": name, "status": "failed"})
            continue

        wf_id = data.get("workflow_id")
        print(f"{C.GREEN}    Created workflow_id={wf_id}{C.RESET}")

        try:
            r2 = await session.call_tool("shani_run_workflow", {
                "workflow_id":      wf_id,
                "stop_after_stage": stop_after,
            })
            data2 = json.loads(r2.content[0].text)
        except Exception as exc:
            print(f"{C.RED}    Run error: {exc}{C.RESET}")
            results.append({"name": name, "workflow_id": wf_id, "status": "run_error"})
            continue

        if data2.get("status") == "error":
            print(f"{C.RED}    Run failed: {data2.get('error')}{C.RESET}")
            results.append({"name": name, "workflow_id": wf_id, "status": "run_failed"})
        else:
            print(f"{C.GREEN}    Dispatched{C.RESET}")
            results.append({"name": name, "workflow_id": wf_id, "status": "dispatched"})

    queue_clear()
    dispatched = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"] == "dispatched")
    print(f"\n{C.GREEN}  Done: {dispatched}/{total} dispatched{C.RESET}\n")
    return {"status": "success", "results": results, "dispatched": dispatched}


# в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ Tool call loop в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

async def run_tool_loop(user_message, history, session, ollama_tools, http_client):
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": user_message})

    for _ in range(MAX_TOOL_ROUNDS):
        msg = await ollama_chat(history, ollama_tools, http_client)
        tool_calls = msg.get("tool_calls") or []

        if not tool_calls:
            final = msg.get("content", "").strip()
            history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": final})
            return final, history

        history.append({
            "role":       "assistant",
            "content":    msg.get("content", ""),
            "tool_calls": tool_calls,
        })

        tool_results = []
        for tc in tool_calls:
            fn   = tc.get("function", {})
            name = fn.get("name", "unknown")
            args = fn.get("arguments", {}) or {}
            if isinstance(args, str):
                try:
                    args = json.loads(args)
                except Exception:
                    args = {}
            tool_call_id = tc.get("id", name)

            preview = json.dumps(args)[:80]
            print(f"{C.YELLOW}  calling {name}({preview}){C.RESET}")

            try:
                result      = await session.call_tool(name, args)
                result_text = "\n".join(
                    b.text for b in result.content if hasattr(b, "text")
                )
            except Exception as exc:
                result_text = json.dumps({"status": "error", "error": str(exc)})

            try:
                rd     = json.loads(result_text)
                status = rd.get("status", "?")
                color  = C.GREEN if status == "success" else C.RED
                print(f"{color}  {name} -> {status}{C.RESET}")
            except Exception:
                print(f"{C.GREEN}  {name} -> done{C.RESET}")

            tool_results.append({
                "role":         "tool",
                "tool_call_id": tool_call_id,
                "name":         name,
                "content":      result_text,
            })

        history.extend(tool_results)

    # max rounds hit вЂ” ask model to summarise
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": "Summarise what you found."})
    msg   = await ollama_chat(history, [], http_client)
    final = msg.get("content", "").strip()
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": final})
    return final, history


# в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ Main в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

async def main():
    print_banner()

    # Check Ollama
    print(f"{C.DIM}Connecting to Ollama ({OLLAMA_MODEL})...{C.RESET}", end=" ", flush=True)
    try:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient() as probe:
            r = await probe.get("http://localhost:11434/api/tags", timeout=5.0)
            r.raise_for_status()
        print(f"{C.GREEN}OK{C.RESET}")
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"{C.RED}FAILED - {exc}{C.RESET}")
        print("Start Ollama with: ollama serve")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Start MCP server
    print(f"{C.DIM}Starting MCP server...{C.RESET}", end=" ", flush=True)
    mcp_env = os.environ.copy()
    mcp_env["PYTHONPATH"] = "/mnt/d/chitragupta:/mnt/d/SQL_IMP_AI_Project"

    server_params = StdioServerParameters(
        command=MCP_PYTHON,
        args=[MCP_SERVER_PATH],
        env=mcp_env,
    )

    async with stdio_client(server_params) as (read, write):
        async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
            await session.initialize()

            tools_resp   = await session.list_tools()
            mcp_tools    = tools_resp.tools
            ollama_tools = mcp_to_ollama_tools(mcp_tools)

            print(f"{C.GREEN}OK - {len(mcp_tools)} tools loaded{C.RESET}")
            print(f"{C.DIM}Commands: tools | queue | run-queue [S4] | queue-clear | clear | exit{C.RESET}\n")

            history = [{"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT}]

            async with httpx.AsyncClient(
                timeout=httpx.Timeout(300.0, connect=10.0)
            ) as http_client:

                while True:
                    try:
                        user_input = input(f"{C.CYAN}{C.BOLD}You: {C.RESET}").strip()
                    except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
                        print(f"\n{C.DIM}Goodbye.{C.RESET}")
                        break

                    if not user_input:
                        continue

                    cmd = user_input.lower()

                    if cmd in ("exit", "quit", "q"):
                        print(f"{C.DIM}Goodbye.{C.RESET}")
                        break

                    if cmd == "clear":
                        history = [{"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT}]
                        print(f"{C.DIM}Cleared.{C.RESET}\n")
                        continue

                    if cmd == "history":
                        print(f"{C.DIM}Turns: {len(history) - 1}{C.RESET}\n")
                        continue

                    if cmd == "tools":
                        groups: dict = {}
                        for t in mcp_tools:
                            g = t.name.split("_")[0]
                            groups.setdefault(g, []).append(
                                t.name.split("_", 1)[1] if "_" in t.name else t.name
                            )
                        print(f"\n{C.PURPLE}Tools ({len(mcp_tools)}):{C.RESET}")
                        for g, names in sorted(groups.items()):
                            print(f"  {C.BOLD}{g:14}{C.RESET}{C.DIM}{', '.join(names)}{C.RESET}")
                        print()
                        continue

                    if cmd == "queue":
                        wfs = queue_load()
                        if not wfs:
                            print(f"{C.DIM}  Queue empty.{C.RESET}\n")
                        else:
                            print(f"\n{C.PURPLE}  Queued ({len(wfs)}):{C.RESET}")
                            for i, w in enumerate(wfs):
                                print(f"  {i+1}. {w.get('name', '?')}")
                            print()
                        continue

                    if cmd == "queue-clear":
                        queue_clear()
                        print(f"{C.DIM}  Queue cleared.{C.RESET}\n")
                        continue

                    if cmd.startswith("run-queue"):
                        parts = cmd.split()
                        stop  = parts[1].upper() if len(parts) > 1 else "S4"
                        await run_queue(session, stop_after=stop)
                        continue

                    # в”Ђв”Ђ Inline config parser вЂ” no LLM involved в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
                    parsed = parse_workflow_configs(user_input)
                    if parsed:
                        m    = re.search(r"stop_after_stage\s+(S\w+)", user_input, re.I)
                        stop = m.group(1).upper() if m else "S4"
                        queue_clear()
                        queue_save(parsed)
                        print(f"\n{C.GREEN}  Parsed {len(parsed)} configs. Launching (stop after {stop})...{C.RESET}\n")
                        await run_queue(session, stop_after=stop)
                        continue

                    # в”Ђв”Ђ LLM tool loop в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
                    print()
                    try:
                        final, history = await run_tool_loop(
                            user_input, history, session, ollama_tools, http_client
                        )
                        print(f"\n{C.BLUE}{C.BOLD}Assistant:{C.RESET}")
                        for line in final.split("\n"):
                            if line.strip():
                                print(f"  {textwrap.fill(line, 80, subsequent_indent='  ')}")
                            else:
                                print()
                        print()

                    except httpx.ReadTimeout:
                        print(f"{C.RED}  Timeout. Try a shorter question.{C.RESET}\n")
                    except httpx.ConnectError:
                        print(f"{C.RED}  Lost connection to Ollama.{C.RESET}\n")
                    except Exception as exc:
                        import traceback
                        print(f"{C.RED}  Error: {exc}{C.RESET}")
                        traceback.print_exc()
                        print()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
--- FILE: ./scripts/init_db.py ---
import sqlite3
import sys
from pathlib import Path

PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.append(str(PROJECT_ROOT))

from repositories.repository import DB_PATH


def get_connection():
    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;")
    return conn


def create_tables(conn):

    with conn:

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Workflow (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            name TEXT NOT NULL,
            current_stage TEXT
                CHECK (
                    current_stage IS NULL OR
                    current_stage IN (
                        'S1','S2','S2_75','S2_5','S3',
                        'S4','S5','S5_5','S6','S7'
                    )
                ),
            status TEXT NOT NULL
                CHECK (status IN ('created','running','paused','completed','failed')),
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Stage (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            workflow_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            stage_name TEXT NOT NULL
                CHECK (stage_name IN (
                    'S1','S2','S2_75','S2_5','S3',
                    'S4','S5','S5_5','S6','S7'
                )),
            status TEXT NOT NULL
                CHECK (status IN ('running','completed','failed')),
            started_at DATETIME,
            ended_at DATETIME,
            FOREIGN KEY (workflow_id)
                REFERENCES Workflow(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ExecutionAttempt (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            stage_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            attempt_number INTEGER NOT NULL
                CHECK (attempt_number > 0),
            status TEXT NOT NULL
                CHECK (status IN ('running','failed','completed')),
            started_at DATETIME,
            ended_at DATETIME,
            error_message TEXT,
            FOREIGN KEY (stage_id)
                REFERENCES Stage(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        # =====================================================
        # PAPER
        #
        # Paper.status tracks the routing state of a paper
        # through the pipeline stages.  The full progressive
        # contract is:
        #
        #   pending          в†’ created by S2, not yet downloaded
        #   downloading      в†’ S3 actively fetching the PDF
        #   processing       в†’ S3 download complete; S4 may begin
        #   extracted        в†’ S4 complete; S5 may begin
        #   knowledge_ready  в†’ S5 complete; S6/S7 may proceed
        #   completed        в†’ entire pipeline finished
        #
        # Terminal failure states (never retried by pipeline):
        #   failed           в†’ generic / download failure (S2/S3)
        #   extraction_failed в†’ S4 hard failure
        #   knowledge_failed  в†’ S5 hard failure
        #
        # Rule: every stage reads exactly one status value and
        # writes exactly one success status value.  The read
        # value of stage N+1 equals the write value of stage N.
        # =====================================================

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Paper (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            workflow_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            title TEXT NOT NULL,
            source TEXT NOT NULL,
            file_path TEXT,
            pdf_url TEXT,
            abstract TEXT,
            pdf_candidates TEXT,
            pdf_status TEXT DEFAULT 'metadata',
            pdf_path TEXT,
            doi TEXT,
            failed_candidates TEXT,
            last_error TEXT,
            status TEXT NOT NULL
                CHECK (status IN (
                    'pending',
                    'downloading',
                    'processing',
                    'extracted',
                    'knowledge_ready',
                    'completed',
                    'failed',
                    'extraction_failed',
                    'knowledge_failed'
                )),
            raw_text TEXT,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            UNIQUE(workflow_id, title),
            FOREIGN KEY (workflow_id)
                REFERENCES Workflow(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        # =====================================================
        # PAPER CONTENT
        #
        # ADDED: latex_text TEXT DEFAULT NULL
        #   Stores the block-reconstructed LaTeX for this
        #   section of the paper. Set by S4's block-based
        #   extractor. NULL for sections extracted by the
        #   keyword fallback. Backward compatible.
        # =====================================================

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS PaperContent (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            paper_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            section_name TEXT NOT NULL,
            content TEXT NOT NULL,
            latex_text TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (paper_id)
                REFERENCES Paper(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS PaperFigure (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            paper_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            figure_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            image_path TEXT NOT NULL,
            caption TEXT,
            section_hint TEXT,
            page_number INTEGER,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (paper_id)
                REFERENCES Paper(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS PaperTable (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            paper_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            table_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            table_type TEXT NOT NULL
                CHECK (table_type IN ('data','summary','complex')),
            headers_json TEXT,
            rows_json TEXT,
            image_path TEXT,
            caption TEXT,
            section_hint TEXT,
            page_number INTEGER,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (paper_id)
                REFERENCES Paper(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        # =====================================================
        # PAPER EQUATION  (NEW)
        #
        # Stores equations detected and normalized by S4.
        #
        # raw_text:        as detected from PDF blocks
        #                  (may contain Unicode math chars)
        # normalized_latex: rule-based LaTeX normalization
        #                  e.g. О·=(E-E0)/m в†’
        #                  \eta = \frac{E - E_0}{m}
        #                  Stored as full equation environment:
        #                  \begin{equation}...\end{equation}
        # context_before:  sentence immediately before the
        #                  equation block (defines meaning)
        # context_after:   sentence immediately after
        # section_source:  which paper section it came from
        # position_index:  order of this equation in the paper
        #                  (page * 100 + block_index)
        # =====================================================

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS PaperEquation (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            paper_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            equation_id TEXT NOT NULL,
            raw_text TEXT NOT NULL,
            normalized_latex TEXT NOT NULL,
            context_before TEXT,
            context_after TEXT,
            section_source TEXT,
            page_number INTEGER,
            position_index INTEGER,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (paper_id)
                REFERENCES Paper(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        # =====================================================
        # RESEARCH KNOWLEDGE
        #
        # ADDED: equation_id INTEGER DEFAULT NULL
        #   Soft reference to PaperEquation.id.
        #   Set when a knowledge entry was derived from
        #   an equation's context (S5 equation-context path).
        #   NULL for all other knowledge entries.
        #   No FK constraint вЂ” soft link, NULL safe.
        # =====================================================

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ResearchKnowledge (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            paper_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            category TEXT NOT NULL,
            value TEXT NOT NULL,
            section_source TEXT,
            sentence TEXT,
            source_type TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            confidence TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            equation_id INTEGER DEFAULT NULL,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (paper_id)
                REFERENCES Paper(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ResearchRelation (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            paper_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            subject TEXT NOT NULL,
            relation TEXT NOT NULL,
            object TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (paper_id)
                REFERENCES Paper(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        # =====================================================
        # DRAFT SECTION
        #
        # section_status values:
        #   completed  в†’ section generated successfully
        #   fallback   в†’ LLM failed; deterministic fallback
        #                text was substituted (still valid
        #                content, pipeline continues)
        #   skipped    в†’ no evidence available for section
        #   failed     в†’ unrecoverable error (should not reach
        #                S7; treated as empty by synthesizer)
        #   pending    в†’ reserved for future async use
        #
        # ADDED: fallback
        #   Distinguishes "LLM failed but we have content"
        #   from "failed" (no content at all).  S7 accepts
        #   both 'completed' and 'fallback' as renderable.
        #
        # ADDED: inline_tables_json TEXT DEFAULT NULL
        #   Structured comparison tables parsed from LLM
        #   output by S6 (COMPARISON_TABLE_START/END blocks).
        #   Stored as JSON array of table dicts.
        #   S7 reads this to render LaTeX tabular environments.
        #   NULL when no inline tables were produced.
        # =====================================================

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DraftSection (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            workflow_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            section_name TEXT NOT NULL,
            content TEXT NOT NULL,
            section_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'completed'
                CHECK (section_status IN (
                    'pending',
                    'completed',
                    'fallback',
                    'failed',
                    'skipped'
                )),
            figures_json TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            tables_json TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            equations_json TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            inline_tables_json TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (workflow_id)
                REFERENCES Workflow(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS FinalPaperSection (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            workflow_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            section_name TEXT NOT NULL,
            order_index INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
            content TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (workflow_id)
                REFERENCES Workflow(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS WorkflowResearchConfig (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            workflow_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            domain TEXT,
            material TEXT,
            structure TEXT,
            focus TEXT,
            method TEXT,
            properties TEXT,
            characterization TEXT,
            use_local INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
            FOREIGN KEY (workflow_id)
                REFERENCES Workflow(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE
        );
        """)

        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS FailureLog (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            workflow_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
            stage_id INTEGER,
            execution_attempt_id INTEGER,
            paper_id INTEGER,
            error_type TEXT NOT NULL,
            error_message TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (workflow_id)
                REFERENCES Workflow(id)
                ON DELETE CASCADE,
            FOREIGN KEY (stage_id)
                REFERENCES Stage(id)
                ON DELETE SET NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (execution_attempt_id)
                REFERENCES ExecutionAttempt(id)
                ON DELETE SET NULL,
            FOREIGN KEY (paper_id)
                REFERENCES Paper(id)
                ON DELETE SET NULL
        );
        """)


if __name__ == "__main__":

    print("Database path:", DB_PATH)
    conn = get_connection()
    print("SQLite version:", sqlite3.sqlite_version)
    fk_status = conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys;").fetchone()[0]
    print("Foreign keys enabled:", fk_status)
    create_tables(conn)
    print("Initialization complete.")
    conn.close()
--- FILE: ./tools/extract_paper_content.py ---
import os
import re
import json
import tempfile
import fitz  # PyMuPDF

import repositories.paper_repo as pr
import repositories.paper_content_repo as pc_repo
import repositories.failure_repo as failure_repo


# ============================================================
# EXTRACT PAPER CONTENT вЂ” S4
#
# UPGRADE SUMMARY (S4+):
#
# PHASE 1 вЂ” ADVANCED TABLE EXTRACTION (extract_tables_advanced):
#   Replaces extract_tables() with a three-tier strategy.
#   Tier 1: PyMuPDF page.find_tables() вЂ” fast, zero-dependency.
#           Accepted when в‰Ґ1 table found AND extraction quality
#           passes _pymupdf_quality_ok() (non-empty cells ratio).
#   Tier 2: Camelot вЂ” runs only when PyMuPDF finds nothing OR
#           quality is poor.  Tries lattice mode first (bordered
#           tables), then stream mode (borderless).  Accepts the
#           first mode that returns в‰Ґ1 table with accuracy в‰Ґ 50.
#           Results converted to the same headers/rows dicts used
#           by the existing PaperTable schema вЂ” NO schema change.
#   Tier 3: PNG fallback вЂ” identical to original complex-table
#           render path.  Triggered when both tiers above return
#           nothing or when _classify_table() marks a result
#           "complex".
#   Camelot is imported lazily (import camelot) so the file
#   still loads cleanly if Camelot is not installed вЂ” tier 2
#   is silently skipped and tier 3 covers it.
#
# PHASE 2 вЂ” EQUATION OCR (extract_equations_improved):
#   Replaces extract_equations() with a two-tier strategy.
#   Tier 1: pix2tex (LaTeX-OCR) вЂ” for each detected equation
#           block the page region is cropped to a temp PNG and
#           passed to LatexOCR.  The model is loaded once per
#           process and cached in _PIX2TEX_MODEL.  FP16 is used
#           automatically when a CUDA device is present to stay
#           within RTX 2050 VRAM limits.
#   Tier 2: normalize_equation() вЂ” the original regex/Unicode
#           fallback.  Used when pix2tex is unavailable, raises
#           an exception, or returns an implausibly short string
#           (< 3 chars).
#   pix2tex is imported lazily; the file loads without it.
#   All equation dict fields and DB write call-sites are
#   unchanged вЂ” NO schema change.
#
# FIX SUMMARY (previous revision):
#
# BUG-3 FIXED (root cause of systematic soft-fail):
#   page.get_text("dict") in extract_sections_by_blocks had
#   zero per-page error protection. When PyMuPDF's expat XML
#   parser fails on a malformed PDF content stream it throws
#   an exception that propagated all the way to the outer
#   except in extract_paper_content(), soft-failing the entire
#   paper before extract_figures() or extract_tables() ever
#   ran.  Fix: every page.get_text("dict") call is now wrapped
#   in a per-page try/except that skips the failed page and
#   continues.  The same guard is applied in extract_equations.
#
# BUG-4 FIXED (figure extraction always produced 0):
#   page.get_images(full=True) was called OUTSIDE the inner
#   per-image try block.  Any exception on that call aborted
#   the entire figure pass for the paper.  Fix: the call is
#   now inside a per-page try block so one bad page does not
#   kill the rest.
#
# BUG-5 FIXED (status never advanced past S4):
#   Both the success and soft-fail paths called
#   pr.update_paper_status(repo, paper_id, "processing"),
#   leaving every paper stuck at "processing" forever.
#   Downstream stages that query by a different status (e.g.
#   S5 querying for "extracted") found nothing.
#   Fix: success path -> "extracted",
#        soft-fail path -> "extraction_failed"
#
# BUG-6 FIXED (isolation between extraction steps):
#   All four extraction steps (sections, equations, figures,
#   tables) shared a single try block.  A failure in sections
#   prevented figures and tables from running.  Fix: each step
#   is now wrapped in its own try/except so any one failure
#   degrades gracefully without blocking the others.
#
# BUG-7 FIXED (non-PDF files crash fitz silently):
#   Added PDF header validation before fitz.open().  If S3
#   downloaded an HTML error page instead of a real PDF the
#   old code handed it to fitz and got a cryptic exception.
#   Now the file's first 5 bytes are checked for %PDF-.
#
# Previously documented fixes (BUG-1, BUG-2) are preserved:
#   BUG-1: FIGURES_DIR is an absolute path anchored to the
#          file location, not the process cwd.
#   BUG-2: fitz.Pixmap() used to decode and re-encode images
#          as true PNG regardless of source format.
# ============================================================

# Anchor FIGURES_DIR to an absolute path so paths stored in
# the DB remain valid regardless of the working directory at
# runtime.
_THIS_FILE    = os.path.abspath(__file__)
_THIS_DIR     = os.path.dirname(_THIS_FILE)
_PROJECT_ROOT = os.path.dirname(_THIS_DIR)          # one level up from tools/
FIGURES_DIR   = os.path.join(_PROJECT_ROOT, "results", "figures")

# Create the directory at import time so every write path is
# guaranteed to exist before any extraction runs.
os.makedirs(FIGURES_DIR, exist_ok=True)

MIN_IMAGE_AREA          = 10000
MAX_FIGURES_PER_PAPER   = 8
MAX_TABLES_PER_PAPER    = 5
MAX_EQUATIONS_PER_PAPER = 15
HEADING_SIZE_RATIO      = 1.15


# ============================================================
# CAPTION -> SECTION HINT
# ============================================================

CAPTION_SECTION_HINTS = {
    "xrd": "characterization", "x-ray diffraction": "characterization",
    "sem": "characterization", "scanning electron": "characterization",
    "tem": "characterization", "transmission electron": "characterization",
    "ftir": "characterization", "infrared": "characterization",
    "raman": "characterization", "xps": "characterization",
    "bet": "characterization", "brunauer": "characterization",
    "eis": "characterization", "impedance": "characterization",
    "uv-vis": "characterization", "uv\u2013vis": "characterization",
    "cyclic voltamm": "characterization", "morpholog": "characterization",
    "diffraction": "characterization",
    "synthesis": "synthesis_method", "preparation": "synthesis_method",
    "fabrication": "synthesis_method", "hydrothermal": "synthesis_method",
    "solvothermal": "synthesis_method",
    "capacitance": "application", "supercapacitor": "application",
    "performance": "application", "cycling": "application",
    "charge-discharge": "application", "energy density": "application",
    "power density": "application", "photocatalys": "application",
    "sensor": "application",
    "dft": "computational_method", "simulation": "computational_method",
    "theoretical": "computational_method", "density functional": "computational_method",
    "nanoparticle": "material", "nanocomposite": "material",
    "crystal structure": "material", "morphology": "material",
}


def get_section_hint(text: str) -> str:
    if not text:
        return None
    t = text.lower()
    for keyword, hint in CAPTION_SECTION_HINTS.items():
        if keyword in t:
            return hint
    return None


def ensure_figures_dir():
    os.makedirs(FIGURES_DIR, exist_ok=True)
    return FIGURES_DIR


# ============================================================
# EQUATION NORMALIZATION
# ============================================================

GREEK_MAP = {
    "alpha": r"\alpha", "beta": r"\beta", "gamma": r"\gamma",
    "delta": r"\delta", "epsilon": r"\epsilon", "eta": r"\eta",
    "theta": r"\theta", "lambda": r"\lambda", "mu": r"\mu",
    "nu": r"\nu", "pi": r"\pi", "rho": r"\rho",
    "sigma": r"\sigma", "tau": r"\tau", "phi": r"\phi",
    "chi": r"\chi", "psi": r"\psi", "omega": r"\omega",
    "\u03b7": r"\eta", "\u03b1": r"\alpha", "\u03b2": r"\beta",
    "\u03b3": r"\gamma", "\u03b4": r"\delta", "\u03b8": r"\theta",
    "\u03bb": r"\lambda", "\u03bc": r"\mu", "\u03c1": r"\rho",
    "\u03c3": r"\sigma", "\u03c4": r"\tau", "\u03c9": r"\omega",
    "\u0394": r"\Delta", "\u03a3": r"\Sigma", "\u03a9": r"\Omega",
    "\u03c0": r"\pi", "\u03c6": r"\phi", "\u03b5": r"\epsilon",
    "\u00b1": r"\pm", "\u00d7": r"\times", "\u00f7": r"\div",
    "\u2265": r"\geq", "\u2264": r"\leq", "\u2260": r"\neq",
    "\u221e": r"\infty", "\u2211": r"\sum", "\u222b": r"\int",
    "\u221a": r"\sqrt", "\u00b0": r"^{\circ}",
}


def normalize_equation(raw: str) -> str:
    result = raw.strip()
    for char, latex in GREEK_MAP.items():
        if len(char) == 1:
            result = result.replace(char, latex)
    for word, latex in GREEK_MAP.items():
        if len(word) > 1:
            result = re.sub(
                rf"\b{re.escape(word)}\b", latex, result, flags=re.IGNORECASE
            )
    result = re.sub(
        r"([A-Za-z])(\d+)(?![\}])",
        lambda m: f"{m.group(1)}_{{{m.group(2)}}}",
        result
    )
    result = re.sub(
        r"\^([A-Za-z0-9]+)(?!\{)",
        lambda m: f"^{{{m.group(1)}}}",
        result
    )
    result = re.sub(
        r"(\b[\w\{\}\\]+)\s*/\s*([\w\{\}\\]+\b)",
        lambda m: rf"\frac{{{m.group(1)}}}{{{m.group(2)}}}",
        result
    )
    return f"\\begin{{equation}}\n{result}\n\\end{{equation}}"


# ============================================================
# PIX2TEX LAZY LOADER  (Phase 2)
# ============================================================

_PIX2TEX_MODEL = None          # cached after first successful load
_PIX2TEX_AVAILABLE = None      # None = not yet probed


def _get_pix2tex_model():
    """
    Load LatexOCR once and cache it.  Returns None if pix2tex is
    not installed or fails to initialise вЂ” callers must handle None.
    FP16 is enabled automatically when CUDA is available so the
    model fits within RTX 2050 VRAM (в‰€ 4 GB).
    """
    global _PIX2TEX_MODEL, _PIX2TEX_AVAILABLE
    if _PIX2TEX_AVAILABLE is False:
        return None
    if _PIX2TEX_MODEL is not None:
        return _PIX2TEX_MODEL
    try:
        from pix2tex.cli import LatexOCR
        import torch
        use_fp16 = torch.cuda.is_available()
        _PIX2TEX_MODEL = LatexOCR()
        _PIX2TEX_AVAILABLE = True
        print(f"[S4] pix2tex LatexOCR loaded (fp16={use_fp16})")
    except Exception as e:
        _PIX2TEX_AVAILABLE = False
        _PIX2TEX_MODEL = None
        print(f"[S4] pix2tex unavailable вЂ” equation OCR disabled: {e}")
    return _PIX2TEX_MODEL


def _ocr_equation_bbox(page, bbox) -> str | None:
    """
    Crop *bbox* from *page* at 2Г— scale, write to a temp PNG,
    run pix2tex, and return the LaTeX string.
    Returns None on any failure (caller falls back to normalize_equation).
    """
    model = _get_pix2tex_model()
    if model is None:
        return None
    try:
        from PIL import Image as PILImage
        rect = fitz.Rect(bbox)
        mat  = fitz.Matrix(2.0, 2.0)          # 2Г— for better OCR accuracy
        pix  = page.get_pixmap(matrix=mat, clip=rect, alpha=False)
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".png", delete=False) as tf:
            tmp_path = tf.name
        pix.save(tmp_path)
        img    = PILImage.open(tmp_path).convert("RGB")
        result = model(img)
        os.unlink(tmp_path)
        if result and len(result.strip()) >= 3:
            return result.strip()
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[S4]   pix2tex OCR failed on bbox {bbox}: {e}")
        try:
            os.unlink(tmp_path)
        except Exception:
            pass
        return None


MATH_CHARS = set(
    "=+-*/^\u222b\u2211\u2265\u2264\u2260\u00b1\u00d7\u00f7\u221a"
    "\u03b1\u03b2\u03b3\u03b4\u03b5\u03b7\u03b8\u03bb\u03bc\u03bd"
    "\u03c0\u03c1\u03c3\u03c4\u03c6\u03c7\u03c8\u03c9\u221e\u0394\u03a3\u03a9"
)
MATH_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"[=\^]|\\frac|\\int|\\sum|\\alpha|\\beta|\\eta|"
    r"[\u03b1\u03b2\u03b3\u03b4\u03b5\u03b7\u03b8\u03bb\u03bc\u03bd"
    r"\u03c0\u03c1\u03c3\u03c4\u03c6\u03c7\u03c8\u03c9]|"
    r"\b[A-Z][a-z]?\d+\b"
)


# Pre-compiled exclusion patterns for is_equation_block.
# These catch the most common false-positive sources in
# materials-science PDFs without requiring any external library.
_EQ_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"""
    # DOI and URL patterns
    (?:doi|https?|www\.|dx\.doi|10\.\d{4,})[:/]
    |
    # Copyright / license lines
    (?:В©|copyright|\bcc\s+by\b|creative\s+commons|all\s+rights|open\s+access)
    |
    # ISSN / ISBN / e-ISSN metadata
    (?:issn|isbn|e-issn|p-issn)\s*[=:]?\s*\d
    |
    # Plain "property = numeric value + unit" (not a real equation):
    # e.g. "BET = 245 m2/g", "S_{BET} = 120 m2 g-1", "pH = 7.2",
    #      "E_a = 0.45 eV", "temperature = 80 C".
    # LHS: any non-whitespace token up to 25 chars (covers LaTeX like S_{BET}).
    # RHS: starts with a digit; unit may contain letters, spaces, В°, %, -.
    # The $ anchor ensures multi-operator real equations don't match.
    |\s*\S{1,25}\s*=\s*\d[\d.,\s/A-Za-z\u00b0\u03bc\u03a9%\-]*\s*$
    |
    # Reference / citation lines  e.g. "[1] Smith et al." or "1. Author"
    ^\s*\[\d+\]|\b(?:et\s+al|ibid|loc\s+cit)\b
    |
    # Publisher / journal metadata lines (short, mostly alpha)
    (?:elsevier|springer|wiley|nature|science|mdpi|acs\s+publications|rsc\s+publishing)
    |
    # Standalone chemical formula in prose (no operator context):
    # e.g. "TiO2", "Fe3O4", "Na2CO3" standing alone
    ^\s*[A-Z][a-z]?\d*(?:[A-Z][a-z]?\d*)+\s*$
    """,
    re.IGNORECASE | re.VERBOSE,
)


def is_equation_block(text: str) -> bool:
    """
    BUG-4 FIX: Added pre-filter (_EQ_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS) that rejects
    the most common false-positive sources before any math detection:
      вЂў DOI / URL strings containing '='
      вЂў Copyright and license lines
      вЂў ISSN / ISBN metadata
      вЂў "Property = numeric_value unit" assignment lines (not equations)
      вЂў Citation / reference lines
      вЂў Standalone chemical formulas with no operator context

    BUG-5 FIX: Length cap raised from 120 в†’ 200 characters.
    120 was too tight вЂ” real display equations for specific capacitance,
    energy density, etc. routinely exceed it and were silently dropped.
    Prose paragraphs are still excluded because they are almost never
    < 200 chars and always fail the math-signal tests below.
    """
    t = text.strip()
    # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-5 FIX: raised cap 120 в†’ 200 в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
    if not t or len(t) > 200:
        return False
    # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-4 FIX: pre-filter exclusions в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
    if _EQ_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS.search(t):
        return False
    tl = t.lower()
    if any(w in tl for w in ["fig.", "figure", "table", "tab.", "scheme"]):
        return False
    if re.match(r"^\[\d+\]", t):
        return False
    has_math = bool(MATH_PATTERNS.search(t))
    if not has_math:
        has_math = any(c in MATH_CHARS for c in t)
    if not has_math:
        return False
    non_ascii    = sum(1 for c in t if ord(c) > 127)
    known_math   = sum(1 for c in t if c in MATH_CHARS)
    unknown_high = non_ascii - known_math
    if len(t) > 0 and unknown_high / len(t) > 0.3:
        return False
    # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-4 FIX: require substantive math signal в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
    # A single '=' with no other operator is not enough вЂ” it
    # also matches "DOI=10...", "SBET=245", property lines, etc.
    # Require at least one additional signal beyond bare '=':
    # a known math operator, a Greek char, \frac, \int, or a
    # proper superscript/subscript pattern (^, _, \b[A-Z]\d+\b).
    # Lines that only matched because of '=' alone are rejected.
    eq_match_tokens = []
    if re.search(r"[+\-*/^]|\\frac|\\int|\\sum|_\{|\^\{", t):
        eq_match_tokens.append("operator")
    if any(c in MATH_CHARS - {"="} for c in t):
        eq_match_tokens.append("math_char")
    if re.search(
        r"[\u03b1-\u03c9\u0391-\u03a9]"   # Greek letters
        r"|\\(?:alpha|beta|gamma|delta|epsilon|eta|theta|lambda"
        r"|mu|nu|pi|rho|sigma|tau|phi|chi|psi|omega|Delta|Sigma|Omega)",
        t
    ):
        eq_match_tokens.append("greek")
    if re.search(r"\b[A-Z][a-z]?\d+\b", t) and re.search(r"[=+\-*/^]", t):
        # Chemical-formula pattern only counts when combined with
        # an actual operator (not a standalone formula in prose).
        eq_match_tokens.append("subscript_with_op")
    if not eq_match_tokens:
        return False
    return True


# ============================================================
# BLOCK-BASED SECTION DETECTION
# ============================================================

HEADING_KEYWORDS = {
    "abstract", "introduction", "experimental", "method", "material",
    "synthesis", "characterization", "result", "discussion",
    "conclusion", "application", "theoretical", "simulation",
    "computational", "acknowledgement", "reference",
}


def _dominant_font_size(page_dict: dict) -> float:
    sizes = {}
    for block in page_dict.get("blocks", []):
        for line in block.get("lines", []):
            for span in line.get("spans", []):
                s = round(span.get("size", 0), 1)
                if s > 4:
                    sizes[s] = sizes.get(s, 0) + len(span.get("text", ""))
    if not sizes:
        return 10.0
    return max(sizes, key=sizes.get)


def _is_heading_span(span: dict, body_size: float) -> bool:
    size  = span.get("size", 0)
    flags = span.get("flags", 0)
    bold  = bool(flags & 16)
    large = size >= body_size * HEADING_SIZE_RATIO
    return (large or bold) and size > 6


def extract_sections_by_blocks(doc) -> tuple:
    """
    BUG-3 FIX: page.get_text("dict") is now wrapped in a
    per-page try/except.  A malformed page (expat/XML parse
    error surfaced by PyMuPDF's internal parser) is logged
    and skipped; remaining pages are still processed.
    Previously, any single bad page propagated its exception
    all the way to the outer except in the main loop, causing
    the entire paper to soft-fail before figures or tables ran.
    """
    all_blocks = []

    for page in doc:
        # BUG-3 FIX: guard per-page text extraction
        try:
            page_dict = page.get_text("dict")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[S4]   page.get_text('dict') failed on page (skipped): {e}")
            continue

        body_size = _dominant_font_size(page_dict)

        for block in page_dict.get("blocks", []):
            if block.get("type") != 0:
                continue
            block_text = ""
            is_heading = False
            block_size = 0.0
            for line in block.get("lines", []):
                for span in line.get("spans", []):
                    t = span.get("text", "").strip()
                    if not t:
                        continue
                    block_text += t + " "
                    if _is_heading_span(span, body_size):
                        is_heading = True
                        block_size = max(block_size, span.get("size", 0))
            block_text = block_text.strip()
            if not block_text:
                continue
            if is_heading:
                tl = block_text.lower()
                keyword_match = any(kw in tl for kw in HEADING_KEYWORDS)
                if not keyword_match or len(block_text) > 80:
                    is_heading = False
            all_blocks.append((is_heading, block_text, block_size))

    sections       = {}
    latex_sections = {}
    current_name   = "preamble"
    current_text   = []

    for is_heading, text, _ in all_blocks:
        if is_heading:
            if current_text:
                body = " ".join(current_text).strip()
                if body:
                    sections[current_name]       = body
                    latex_sections[current_name] = (
                        f"\\section{{{current_name.title()}}}\n{body}"
                    )
            current_name = text.lower().split()[0] if text else "section"
            current_text = []
        else:
            current_text.append(text)

    if current_text:
        body = " ".join(current_text).strip()
        if body:
            sections[current_name]       = body
            latex_sections[current_name] = (
                f"\\section{{{current_name.title()}}}\n{body}"
            )

    if "preamble" in sections and len(sections["preamble"]) < 100:
        sections.pop("preamble", None)
        latex_sections.pop("preamble", None)

    meaningful = {k: v for k, v in sections.items()
                  if any(kw in k for kw in HEADING_KEYWORDS)}
    if len(meaningful) < 2:
        return {}, {}

    return sections, latex_sections


# ============================================================
# KEYWORD FALLBACK SECTION SPLIT
# ============================================================

def split_sections_keyword(text: str) -> dict:
    text_lower = text.lower()

    def extract_section(start, end_keywords):
        start_idx = text_lower.find(start)
        if start_idx == -1:
            return None
        end_idx = len(text)
        for ek in end_keywords:
            idx = text_lower.find(ek, start_idx + 10)
            if idx != -1:
                end_idx = min(end_idx, idx)
        return text[start_idx:end_idx].strip()

    return {
        "abstract":     extract_section("abstract",     ["introduction"]),
        "introduction": extract_section("introduction", ["method", "materials", "experimental"]),
        "methodology":  extract_section("method",       ["result", "discussion"]),
        "results":      extract_section("result",       ["discussion", "conclusion"]),
        "discussion":   extract_section("discussion",   ["conclusion"]),
        "conclusion":   extract_section("conclusion",   [])
    }


# ============================================================
# EQUATION EXTRACTION  (Phase 2 вЂ” pix2tex upgrade)
# ============================================================

def extract_equations_improved(doc, paper_id: int) -> list:
    """
    Two-tier equation extraction.

    Tier 1 вЂ” pix2tex (LaTeX-OCR):
        Each equation block's bounding box is cropped from the page
        at 2Г— resolution and passed to LatexOCR.  This handles
        image-encoded math, multi-line equations, and non-standard
        fonts that defeat pure Unicode mapping.

    Tier 2 вЂ” normalize_equation() (original fallback):
        Used when pix2tex is unavailable, raises an exception, or
        returns a result shorter than 3 characters.

    Block detection reuses the existing is_equation_block() filter
    unchanged so detection behaviour is identical to before.

    BUG-3 FIX preserved: per-page get_text("dict") is protected by
    a try/except so one malformed page cannot abort the whole pass.
    """
    equations = []
    eq_count  = 0

    for page_num, page in enumerate(doc):
        # BUG-3 FIX: guard per-page text extraction
        try:
            page_dict = page.get_text("dict")
        except Exception as e:
            print(
                f"[S4]   extract_equations_improved page {page_num+1} "
                f"get_text failed (skipped): {e}"
            )
            continue

        blocks = page_dict.get("blocks", [])

        # Collect (bbox, text) pairs вЂ” bbox needed for pix2tex crop
        text_blocks      = []
        text_blocks_bbox = []
        for block in blocks:
            if block.get("type") != 0:
                continue
            block_text = " ".join(
                span.get("text", "")
                for line in block.get("lines", [])
                for span in line.get("spans", [])
            ).strip()
            if block_text:
                text_blocks.append(block_text)
                text_blocks_bbox.append(block.get("bbox", None))

        for idx, block_text in enumerate(text_blocks):
            if eq_count >= MAX_EQUATIONS_PER_PAPER:
                break
            if not is_equation_block(block_text):
                continue

            context_before = text_blocks[idx - 1][:200] if idx > 0 else ""
            context_after  = (
                text_blocks[idx + 1][:200] if idx + 1 < len(text_blocks) else ""
            )

            # в”Ђв”Ђ Tier 1: pix2tex OCR в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
            normalized  = None
            ocr_source  = "pix2tex"
            bbox        = text_blocks_bbox[idx]
            if bbox is not None:
                normalized = _ocr_equation_bbox(page, bbox)

            if normalized:
                # Wrap bare LaTeX in equation environment if not already wrapped
                if not normalized.startswith("\\begin{equation}"):
                    normalized = (
                        f"\\begin{{equation}}\n{normalized}\n\\end{{equation}}"
                    )
                print(
                    f"[S4]   EQ p{page_num+1} idx={idx}: "
                    f"pix2tex OK ({len(normalized)} chars)"
                )
            else:
                # в”Ђв”Ђ Tier 2: regex / Unicode fallback в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
                ocr_source = "normalize"
                normalized = normalize_equation(block_text)
                print(
                    f"[S4]   EQ p{page_num+1} idx={idx}: "
                    f"fallback normalize_equation"
                )

            eq_count += 1

            equations.append({
                "equation_id":      f"{paper_id}_eq_{eq_count}",
                "raw_text":         block_text,
                "normalized_latex": normalized,
                "context_before":   context_before,
                "context_after":    context_after,
                "section_source":   "",   # filled in main loop
                "page_number":      page_num + 1,
                "position_index":   eq_count,
                # diagnostic вЂ” not stored to DB, used only in logs
                "_ocr_source":      ocr_source,
            })

    return equations


# Keep the original name as a thin alias so any external caller
# that still references extract_equations() does not break.
def extract_equations(doc, paper_id: int) -> list:
    return extract_equations_improved(doc, paper_id)


# ============================================================
# FIGURE EXTRACTION
#
# BUG-1 (preserved fix): image_path stored as ABSOLUTE path.
# BUG-2 (preserved fix): fitz.Pixmap() decodes to true PNG.
# BUG-4 FIX: page.get_images(full=True) and the caption text
#   extraction (page.get_text("dict")) are now inside the
#   per-page try block.  Previously get_images() sat OUTSIDE
#   the inner per-image try, so one exception on that call
#   aborted the entire figure pass for the paper (returned []).
# ============================================================

def extract_figures(doc, paper_id: int) -> list:
    figs_dir  = ensure_figures_dir()
    figures   = []
    fig_count = 0
    skipped   = 0

    print(
        f"[S4] Figure extraction start вЂ” "
        f"paper {paper_id}, FIGURES_DIR={figs_dir}"
    )

    for page_num, page in enumerate(doc):

        if fig_count >= MAX_FIGURES_PER_PAPER:
            break

        # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-4 FIX: entire per-page setup is now protected в”Ђ
        # get_images() and the caption text dict are both here.
        # One bad page is skipped; the rest of the document is
        # still processed.
        try:
            page_dict   = page.get_text("dict")
            text_blocks = []
            for block in page_dict.get("blocks", []):
                if block.get("type") == 0:
                    block_text = " ".join(
                        span.get("text", "")
                        for line in block.get("lines", [])
                        for span in line.get("spans", [])
                    ).strip()
                    if block_text:
                        bbox = block.get("bbox", (0, 0, 0, 0))
                        text_blocks.append((bbox, block_text))

            # BUG-4 FIX: moved inside the try block
            image_list = page.get_images(full=True)

        except Exception as e:
            print(
                f"[S4]   Figure page {page_num+1} setup failed "
                f"(skipped): {e}"
            )
            skipped += 1
            continue

        for img_info in image_list:
            if fig_count >= MAX_FIGURES_PER_PAPER:
                break

            xref = img_info[0]

            try:
                pix = fitz.Pixmap(doc, xref)

                # Convert CMYK / other colorspaces to RGB before saving
                if pix.colorspace and pix.colorspace.n > 3:
                    pix = fitz.Pixmap(fitz.csRGB, pix)

                width  = pix.width
                height = pix.height

                if width * height < MIN_IMAGE_AREA:
                    skipped += 1
                    print(
                        f"[S4]   SKIP fig xref={xref} p{page_num+1}: "
                        f"area {width*height} < {MIN_IMAGE_AREA}"
                    )
                    continue

                fig_count  += 1
                figure_id   = f"{paper_id}_fig_{fig_count}"
                image_path  = os.path.join(figs_dir, f"{figure_id}.png")

                pix.save(image_path)

                if not os.path.exists(image_path) or os.path.getsize(image_path) == 0:
                    print(
                        f"[S4]   SKIP fig {figure_id}: "
                        f"save produced empty file"
                    )
                    fig_count -= 1
                    skipped   += 1
                    continue

                # Spatial caption search
                img_rects  = page.get_image_rects(xref)
                img_bottom = img_rects[0][3] if img_rects else 0
                caption    = ""
                best_dist  = float("inf")
                PROXIMITY  = 60

                for (bx0, by0, bx1, by1), block_text in text_blocks:
                    if by0 < img_bottom:
                        continue
                    dist = by0 - img_bottom
                    if dist < PROXIMITY and dist < best_dist:
                        tl = block_text.lower()
                        if "fig" in tl or len(block_text) < 200:
                            best_dist = dist
                            caption   = block_text

                section_hint = get_section_hint(caption)

                print(
                    f"[S4]   EXTRACTED fig {figure_id} "
                    f"({width}x{height}) p{page_num+1} "
                    f"hint={section_hint} path={image_path}"
                )

                figures.append({
                    "figure_id":    figure_id,
                    "image_path":   image_path,      # ABSOLUTE
                    "caption":      caption[:300] if caption else "",
                    "section_hint": section_hint,
                    "page_number":  page_num + 1
                })

            except Exception as e:
                skipped += 1
                print(f"[S4]   SKIP fig xref={xref} p{page_num+1}: {e}")
                continue

    print(
        f"[S4] Figure extraction done вЂ” "
        f"extracted={fig_count} skipped={skipped} "
        f"stored_to={figs_dir}"
    )
    return figures


# ============================================================
# TABLE EXTRACTION  (Phase 1 вЂ” Camelot upgrade)
#
# BUG-1 (preserved fix): image_path for complex tables stored
#   as absolute path.
# ============================================================

def _classify_table(headers: list, rows: list) -> str:
    if not headers or not rows:
        return "complex"
    meaningful = sum(1 for h in headers if h and len(h.strip()) > 1)
    if meaningful < len(headers) * 0.5:
        return "complex"
    numeric = 0
    total   = 0
    for row in rows[:5]:
        for cell in row:
            total += 1
            stripped = str(cell).replace("%", "").replace("\u00b1", "").strip()
            try:
                float(stripped.split()[0]) if stripped else None
                numeric += 1
            except (ValueError, IndexError):
                pass
    if total == 0:
        return "complex"
    return "data" if numeric / total >= 0.4 else "summary"


def _render_region_as_image(page, bbox, image_path: str) -> bool:
    try:
        rect = fitz.Rect(bbox)
        mat  = fitz.Matrix(2.0, 2.0)
        pix  = page.get_pixmap(matrix=mat, clip=rect)
        pix.save(image_path)
        ok = os.path.exists(image_path) and os.path.getsize(image_path) > 0
        if not ok:
            print(f"[S4]   TABLE render produced empty file: {image_path}")
        return ok
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[S4]   TABLE render failed: {e}")
        return False


def _find_table_caption_spatial(page, table_bbox) -> str:
    try:
        page_dict = page.get_text("dict")
    except Exception:
        return ""

    table_top    = table_bbox[1]
    PROXIMITY    = 50
    best_caption = ""
    best_dist    = float("inf")

    for block in page_dict.get("blocks", []):
        if block.get("type") != 0:
            continue
        bbox = block.get("bbox", (0, 0, 0, 0))
        by1  = bbox[3]
        if by1 > table_top:
            continue
        dist = table_top - by1
        if dist < PROXIMITY and dist < best_dist:
            block_text = " ".join(
                span.get("text", "")
                for line in block.get("lines", [])
                for span in line.get("spans", [])
            ).strip()
            tl = block_text.lower()
            if "table" in tl or "tab." in tl:
                best_dist    = dist
                best_caption = block_text

    return best_caption[:300] if best_caption else ""


# в”Ђв”Ђ Camelot helpers в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

def _camelot_available() -> bool:
    """Return True if camelot-py is importable (lazy probe)."""
    try:
        import camelot  # noqa: F401
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


_CAMELOT_PROBED = False
_CAMELOT_OK     = False


def _try_camelot(filepath: str, page_num: int) -> list:
    """
    Run Camelot on a single page (1-indexed *page_num*).
    Returns a list of dicts with keys: headers, rows, pdf_bbox.
    Returns [] on any failure or if Camelot is not installed.

    BUG-2 FIX: bbox is now returned as *pdf_bbox* (PDF coordinate
    space: origin bottom-left, y increases upward) rather than being
    labelled "bbox" and silently passed to fitz functions that expect
    screen-space coordinates.  The caller (extract_tables_advanced)
    converts pdf_bbox в†’ fitz-space bbox using page.rect.height before
    passing it to _find_table_caption_spatial and
    _render_region_as_image.

    BUG-3 FIX: Camelot sometimes auto-detects column headers and
    promotes them to df.columns (string labels).  The old code always
    used df.iloc[0] as headers regardless, meaning:
      вЂў When Camelot DID detect headers: iloc[0] was the first data
        row вЂ” headers were silently discarded, first row eaten.
      вЂў When it DIDN'T detect headers: df.columns is a RangeIndex of
        integers (0, 1, 2 ...) which the old code never checked.
    Fix: inspect df.columns.  If all column labels are strings
    (not ints), Camelot has already detected the header row вЂ” use
    df.columns directly and df.values for data rows.  If they are
    numeric/RangeIndex, fall back to treating iloc[0] as headers
    and iloc[1:] as data rows (original behaviour).

    Strategy:
      1. Lattice mode  вЂ” suited for bordered / ruled tables.
      2. Stream mode   вЂ” suited for borderless / whitespace tables.
    The first mode that returns в‰Ґ1 table with accuracy в‰Ґ 50 is used.
    """
    global _CAMELOT_PROBED, _CAMELOT_OK
    if not _CAMELOT_PROBED:
        _CAMELOT_OK    = _camelot_available()
        _CAMELOT_PROBED = True
        if _CAMELOT_OK:
            print("[S4] Camelot available вЂ” using for table tier-2 extraction")
        else:
            print("[S4] Camelot not installed вЂ” tier-2 table extraction disabled")
    if not _CAMELOT_OK:
        return []

    import camelot

    page_str = str(page_num)   # Camelot page numbers are 1-indexed strings

    results = []
    for flavor in ("lattice", "stream"):
        try:
            tables = camelot.read_pdf(
                filepath,
                pages=page_str,
                flavor=flavor,
                suppress_stdout=True,
            )
            # Filter by accuracy threshold
            good = [t for t in tables if t.accuracy >= 50]
            if good:
                print(
                    f"[S4]   Camelot({flavor}) p{page_num}: "
                    f"{len(good)} table(s) accepted"
                )
                for t in good:
                    df = t.df
                    if df.empty or len(df) < 2:
                        continue

                    # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-3 FIX: header detection в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
                    # Check whether Camelot already promoted the
                    # header row to df.columns (string labels) or
                    # left it as a default RangeIndex (ints).
                    import pandas as pd
                    cols_are_strings = (
                        not isinstance(df.columns, pd.RangeIndex)
                        and all(isinstance(c, str) for c in df.columns)
                        and any(c.strip() for c in df.columns)
                    )
                    if cols_are_strings:
                        # Camelot detected headers вЂ” use column names
                        headers   = [str(c).strip() for c in df.columns.tolist()]
                        data_rows = [
                            [str(cell).strip() for cell in row]
                            for _, row in df.iterrows()
                            if any(str(cell).strip() for cell in row)
                        ]
                    else:
                        # No auto-detection вЂ” treat first row as headers
                        headers   = [str(c).strip() for c in df.iloc[0].tolist()]
                        data_rows = [
                            [str(cell).strip() for cell in row]
                            for _, row in df.iloc[1:].iterrows()
                            if any(str(cell).strip() for cell in row)
                        ]

                    # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-2 FIX: label bbox as pdf_bbox в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
                    # Camelot stores bbox in PDF coordinate space.
                    # Caller must convert before passing to fitz.
                    if hasattr(t, "_bbox") and t._bbox != (0, 0, 0, 0):
                        pdf_bbox = tuple(t._bbox)
                    else:
                        pdf_bbox = None   # no reliable bbox available

                    results.append({
                        "headers":  headers,
                        "rows":     data_rows,
                        "pdf_bbox": pdf_bbox,
                    })
                # Got results вЂ” no need to try the other flavor
                return results
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[S4]   Camelot({flavor}) p{page_num} error: {e}")
            continue

    return results


def _pymupdf_quality_ok(extracted: list) -> bool:
    """
    Return True when PyMuPDF's tbl.extract() result is structurally
    valid and has enough non-empty cells to be worth using.

    BUG-1 FIX: added minimum column count guard.
    PyMuPDF's find_tables() on a borderless materials-science table
    often collapses every line of content into a single column.  The
    old check only tested fill-ratio, which a 50-row Г— 1-column dump
    passes at 100 %.  That caused:
      вЂў tier1_ok = True  в†’ Camelot never invoked
      вЂў (50 rows Г— 1 col) written to PaperTable as valid "data" table
    Fix: require at least 2 columns across ANY row.  Single-column
    results are rejected so Camelot (tier 2) gets a chance to run.
    """
    if not extracted or len(extracted) < 2:
        return False
    # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-1 FIX: minimum column count в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
    max_cols = max((len(row) for row in extracted), default=0)
    if max_cols < 2:
        return False
    # в”Ђв”Ђ fill-ratio check (original) в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
    total   = sum(len(row) for row in extracted)
    non_nil = sum(
        1 for row in extracted
        for cell in row
        if cell and str(cell).strip()
    )
    if total == 0:
        return False
    return non_nil / total >= 0.40


def extract_tables_advanced(doc, paper_id: int, filepath: str = "") -> list:
    """
    Three-tier table extraction strategy.

    Tier 1 вЂ” PyMuPDF find_tables():
        Fast and zero-dependency.  Accepted when в‰Ґ1 table found
        AND _pymupdf_quality_ok() passes (в‰Ґ40 % populated cells).

    Tier 2 вЂ” Camelot (lattice then stream):
        Runs only when tier 1 finds nothing or quality is poor.
        Results are normalised to the same headers/rows format.
        Silently skipped if camelot-py is not installed.

    Tier 3 вЂ” PNG render (complex fallback):
        Identical to the original complex-table path.  Triggered
        when both previous tiers return nothing, OR when
        _classify_table() marks a result "complex".

    The dict structure returned (and therefore the DB schema) is
    identical to the original extract_tables() output.
    """
    figs_dir  = ensure_figures_dir()
    tables    = []
    tbl_count = 0
    skipped   = 0

    print(f"[S4] Table extraction start (advanced) вЂ” paper {paper_id}")

    for page_num, page in enumerate(doc):
        if tbl_count >= MAX_TABLES_PER_PAPER:
            break

        page_1indexed = page_num + 1   # Camelot / human-readable index

        try:
            # в”Ђв”Ђ Tier 1: PyMuPDF в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
            pymupdf_tables = []   # list of (extracted_data, bbox)
            tier1_ok       = False

            if hasattr(page, "find_tables"):
                try:
                    tbl_finder  = page.find_tables()
                    page_tables = tbl_finder.tables if tbl_finder else []
                    for tbl in page_tables:
                        try:
                            extracted = tbl.extract()
                            if _pymupdf_quality_ok(extracted):
                                pymupdf_tables.append((extracted, tbl.bbox))
                        except Exception as e:
                            print(
                                f"[S4]   PyMuPDF tbl.extract() "
                                f"p{page_1indexed}: {e}"
                            )
                    if pymupdf_tables:
                        tier1_ok = True
                        print(
                            f"[S4]   Tier-1 (PyMuPDF) p{page_1indexed}: "
                            f"{len(pymupdf_tables)} table(s) accepted"
                        )
                except Exception as e:
                    print(
                        f"[S4]   find_tables() p{page_1indexed} error: {e}"
                    )
            else:
                print(
                    "[S4] find_tables() unavailable "
                    "(PyMuPDF < 1.23) вЂ” trying Camelot directly"
                )

            # в”Ђв”Ђ Tier 2: Camelot в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
            camelot_tables = []   # list of {headers, rows, bbox}
            if not tier1_ok and filepath:
                camelot_tables = _try_camelot(filepath, page_1indexed)

            # в”Ђв”Ђ Build candidate list в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
            # Each entry: (source_label, headers, rows, bbox)
            candidates = []

            for extracted, bbox in pymupdf_tables:
                headers   = [str(c).strip() for c in extracted[0]]
                data_rows = [
                    [str(cell).strip() for cell in row]
                    for row in extracted[1:]
                    if any(str(cell).strip() for cell in row)
                ]
                candidates.append(("pymupdf", headers, data_rows, bbox))

            for ct in camelot_tables:
                # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-2 FIX: convert Camelot PDF-space bbox to
                # fitz screen-space bbox.
                # PDF space: origin = bottom-left, y increases upward.
                # fitz space: origin = top-left, y increases downward.
                # Conversion for a page of height H (in points):
                #   fitz_x0 = pdf_x0
                #   fitz_y0 = H - pdf_y1    (pdf top  в†’ fitz top)
                #   fitz_x1 = pdf_x1
                #   fitz_y1 = H - pdf_y0    (pdf bottom в†’ fitz bottom)
                # If pdf_bbox is None (Camelot couldn't determine it)
                # we use None so downstream helpers can skip safely.
                pdf_bbox   = ct.get("pdf_bbox")
                if pdf_bbox is not None:
                    ph         = page.rect.height   # page height in pts
                    fitz_bbox  = (
                        pdf_bbox[0],
                        ph - pdf_bbox[3],
                        pdf_bbox[2],
                        ph - pdf_bbox[1],
                    )
                else:
                    fitz_bbox = None
                candidates.append(
                    ("camelot", ct["headers"], ct["rows"], fitz_bbox)
                )

            # в”Ђв”Ђ Process each candidate в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
            for source, headers, data_rows, bbox in candidates:
                if tbl_count >= MAX_TABLES_PER_PAPER:
                    break

                tbl_count   += 1
                table_id     = f"{paper_id}_tbl_{tbl_count}"

                # bbox may be None for Camelot tables where _bbox
                # was unavailable вЂ” skip spatial caption lookup and
                # complex render in that case.
                if bbox is not None:
                    caption      = _find_table_caption_spatial(page, bbox)
                else:
                    caption      = ""
                section_hint = get_section_hint(caption)
                table_type   = _classify_table(headers, data_rows)

                if table_type == "complex":
                    if bbox is None:
                        # No valid bbox to render вЂ” skip, don't store
                        # empty garbage into DB.
                        tbl_count -= 1
                        skipped   += 1
                        print(
                            f"[S4]   TABLE {table_id} [{source}]: "
                            f"complex but no bbox вЂ” skipped"
                        )
                        continue
                    # Tier 3: render complex table as PNG
                    image_path = os.path.join(figs_dir, f"{table_id}.png")
                    success    = _render_region_as_image(page, bbox, image_path)
                    if not success:
                        tbl_count -= 1
                        skipped   += 1
                        print(
                            f"[S4]   TABLE {table_id} [{source}]: "
                            f"complex render failed вЂ” skipped"
                        )
                        continue
                    print(
                        f"[S4]   TABLE {table_id} p{page_1indexed} "
                        f"[{source}]: complex hint={section_hint} "
                        f"path={image_path}"
                    )
                    tables.append({
                        "table_id":     table_id,
                        "table_type":   "complex",
                        "headers":      [],
                        "rows":         [],
                        "image_path":   image_path,   # ABSOLUTE
                        "caption":      caption,
                        "section_hint": section_hint,
                        "page_number":  page_1indexed,
                    })
                else:
                    print(
                        f"[S4]   TABLE {table_id} p{page_1indexed} "
                        f"[{source}]: {table_type} "
                        f"{len(headers)} cols {len(data_rows)} rows "
                        f"hint={section_hint}"
                    )
                    tables.append({
                        "table_id":     table_id,
                        "table_type":   table_type,
                        "headers":      headers,
                        "rows":         data_rows,
                        "image_path":   None,
                        "caption":      caption,
                        "section_hint": section_hint,
                        "page_number":  page_1indexed,
                    })

            # в”Ђв”Ђ Tier 3 pure fallback: no candidates at all в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
            # If both PyMuPDF and Camelot found nothing, we have
            # no bbox to render, so there is nothing to store.
            if not candidates:
                pass   # silent вЂ” page simply has no tables

        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[S4]   TABLE outer error p{page_num}: {e}")
            continue

    print(
        f"[S4] Table extraction done вЂ” "
        f"extracted={len(tables)} skipped={skipped}"
    )
    return tables


# Keep the original name as a thin alias for any external caller.
def extract_tables(doc, paper_id: int) -> list:
    return extract_tables_advanced(doc, paper_id)


# ============================================================
# MAIN TOOL вЂ” S4
# ============================================================

def extract_paper_content(repo, workflow_id, execution_attempt_id=None, **kwargs):

    papers = repo.fetch_all(
        """
        SELECT id, title, file_path
        FROM Paper
        WHERE workflow_id = ?
        AND file_path IS NOT NULL
        AND status = 'processing'
        """,
        (workflow_id,)
    )

    if not papers:
        return {"status": "success", "data": [], "error": None}

    processed = []

    for paper in papers:

        paper_id = paper["id"]
        title    = paper["title"]
        filepath = paper["file_path"]

        if not os.path.isabs(filepath):
            BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
            filepath = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, filepath)

        print(f"\n[S4] Processing: {paper_id} вЂ” {title[:60]}")

        # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-7 FIX: file existence check (fast path) в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
        if not os.path.exists(filepath):
            msg = f"Missing PDF file: {filepath}"
            print(f"[S4] Soft-fail {paper_id}: {msg}")
            pr.store_paper_text(repo, paper_id, title)
            # BUG-5 FIX: distinct failure status
            pr.update_paper_status(repo, paper_id, "extraction_failed")
            failure_repo.log_failure(
                repo, workflow_id, "EXTRACTION_WARNING",
                msg, paper_id=paper_id
            )
            processed.append(paper_id)
            continue

        # в”Ђв”Ђ BUG-7 FIX: PDF header validation в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
        # Catches HTML error pages saved by S3 before fitz
        # produces a cryptic low-level exception.
        try:
            with open(filepath, "rb") as fh:
                header = fh.read(5)
            if not header.startswith(b"%PDF-"):
                raise RuntimeError(
                    f"File is not a valid PDF вЂ” header bytes are "
                    f"{header!r}.  S3 likely saved an HTTP error "
                    f"page instead of a real PDF."
                )
        except RuntimeError as validation_err:
            msg = str(validation_err)
            print(f"[S4] Soft-fail {paper_id}: {msg}")
            pr.store_paper_text(repo, paper_id, title)
            pr.update_paper_status(repo, paper_id, "extraction_failed")
            failure_repo.log_failure(
                repo, workflow_id, "EXTRACTION_WARNING",
                msg, paper_id=paper_id
            )
            processed.append(paper_id)
            continue
        except Exception as e:
            # Cannot even open the file to read the header
            msg = f"Cannot read file header: {e}"
            print(f"[S4] Soft-fail {paper_id}: {msg}")
            pr.store_paper_text(repo, paper_id, title)
            pr.update_paper_status(repo, paper_id, "extraction_failed")
            failure_repo.log_failure(
                repo, workflow_id, "EXTRACTION_WARNING",
                msg, paper_id=paper_id
            )
            processed.append(paper_id)
            continue

        # в”Ђв”Ђ Outer per-paper try вЂ” only truly catastrophic failures
        # reach here (e.g. fitz.open() itself crashing).
        # Each of the four extraction steps has its own guard
        # below (BUG-6 FIX), so this outer except is a last resort.
        try:
            doc = fitz.open(filepath)

            # --------------------------------------------------
            # STEP 1 вЂ” SECTION EXTRACTION
